description: Hardening round 2: fix silent error swallowing (bare except, AuthContext, background tasks), non-atomic operations (bulk edit, AI quota), blocking UI patterns, missing input validation, localStorage crash in private browsing, and KanbanBoard render performance.

userStories.title: Replace bare except Exception with specific catches in seo/router.py
userStories.description: backend/seo/router.py has two bare `except Exception` blocks: line 49 in _get_latest_job_lastmod (silently returns today's date) and line 64 in _get_active_share_slugs (silently returns empty list). Replace with specific exception types (e.g., PyMongoError) and add structlog error logging so production failures are visible in monitoring.
acceptanceCriteria: No bare `except Exception` in seo/router.py
Each catch block uses a specific exception type (e.g., PyMongoError, ValueError)
Each catch block logs via structlog with exc_info=True
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Silent failures make sitemap/robots.txt serve stale data with no monitoring visibility.

userStories.title: Replace bare except Exception in tier_check.py and service_ai.py
userStories.description: backend/middleware/tier_check.py has bare except Exception at lines 38 (_get_user_tier returns 'free') and 52 (_count_resource returns 0). backend/applications/service_ai.py line 28 catches Exception and silently returns body. Replace all three with specific exception types and structlog logging.
acceptanceCriteria: No bare `except Exception` in tier_check.py or service_ai.py
Each catch uses specific exception types (PyMongoError, ValueError, etc.)
All catch blocks log via structlog with context (user_id, resource type, etc.)
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: tier_check silently falling back to 'free' could let paying users lose features; service_ai silently skipping AI parsing loses data.

userStories.title: Add error handler to fire-and-forget AI scoring background task
userStories.description: backend/applications/service.py lines 174-179: asyncio.create_task(_score_and_update(...)) is fire-and-forget — any exception is silently swallowed. Wrap the task body in a try/except that logs errors via structlog. The task should catch Exception (not just QuotaExceededError), log it with app_id and user_id context, and not re-raise.
acceptanceCriteria: Background scoring task has try/except wrapping the full operation
Caught exceptions are logged via structlog with app_id and user_id
QuotaExceededError is still handled gracefully (no error log for expected quota hits)
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Without this, failed fit scores are invisible — users never get scores and nobody knows why.

userStories.title: Make AI quota check-and-increment atomic with findOneAndUpdate
userStories.description: backend/parsing/ai_cache.py lines 138-149: check_and_increment_quota() does a non-atomic read-check-increment sequence. Two concurrent requests can both pass the quota check before either increments. Replace with MongoDB findOneAndUpdate: first reset the date if changed, then use update_one with a filter condition {fit_scores_today: {$lt: limit}} and $inc to atomically check-and-increment in one operation.
acceptanceCriteria: Quota check and increment happen in a single atomic MongoDB operation
Date reset (when day changes) is also atomic
Concurrent requests cannot both exceed the quota limit
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Race condition allows users to exceed daily AI limits. Low probability but real.

userStories.title: Make bulk edit tags_add + tags_remove atomic in single update_many
userStories.description: backend/applications/service_bulk.py lines 72-99: when both tags_add and tags_remove are specified, two separate update_many calls are made. If the second fails, tags are added but not removed. MongoDB does not allow $addToSet and $pull in the same update document, so the fix is: run $addToSet first, then $pull second, but wrap both in a single MongoDB client session (transaction) using Motor's async with await client.start_session().
acceptanceCriteria: When both tags_add and tags_remove are specified, operations use a MongoDB session/transaction
If either operation fails, both are rolled back
Single-operation cases (only add OR only remove) still work without transaction overhead
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Data integrity issue — inconsistent tag state on partial failure.

userStories.title: Add try-catch to localStorage calls in Dashboard.jsx
userStories.description: frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx line 27 calls localStorage.getItem() and line 40 calls localStorage.setItem() without try-catch. In private browsing mode or when storage quota is exceeded, these throw errors that crash the component. Wrap both in try-catch: getItem should fall back to 'list' default, setItem should log a warning and continue.
acceptanceCriteria: localStorage.getItem wrapped in try-catch, falls back to 'list' on error
localStorage.setItem wrapped in try-catch, logs console.warn on error
Dashboard renders correctly when localStorage is unavailable
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: Crashes Dashboard in private browsing mode — common user scenario.

userStories.title: Log AuthContext fetchCurrentUser errors instead of silently swallowing
userStories.description: frontend/src/context/AuthContext.jsx line 16: .catch(() => {}) silently swallows all errors from fetchCurrentUser(). Replace with .catch((err) => console.error('[auth] Failed to fetch current user:', err.message)) so auth failures are visible in browser console for debugging. Do NOT change the behavior — user should still see unauthenticated state, just with a log.
acceptanceCriteria: AuthContext .catch() logs the error message via console.error
Log includes '[auth]' prefix for filtering
Auth failure still results in unauthenticated state (no crash)
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: Silent auth failures are the #1 debugging headache — this makes them visible.

userStories.title: Replace blocking window.confirm with non-blocking pattern in useDetailPanelState
userStories.description: frontend/src/hooks/useDetailPanelState.js line 10: window.confirm('Discard unsaved notes?') blocks the UI thread. Replace with a state-driven pattern: add a showDiscardDialog boolean state, and instead of calling window.confirm, set it to true. The consuming component (DetailPanel) should render a confirmation modal when showDiscardDialog is true, with 'Discard' and 'Cancel' buttons. Export showDiscardDialog, confirmDiscard, and cancelDiscard from the hook.
acceptanceCriteria: No window.confirm() calls in useDetailPanelState.js
Hook exports showDiscardDialog state, confirmDiscard(), and cancelDiscard()
DetailPanel renders a confirmation dialog when showDiscardDialog is true
Discard button calls confirmDiscard (closes panel), Cancel calls cancelDiscard (stays open)
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: Blocking dialogs are poor UX, especially on mobile.

userStories.title: Add date_from/date_to validation with 400 error in jobs/router.py
userStories.description: backend/jobs/router.py line 62: datetime.fromisoformat(date_from) is called without try-catch. Malformed date strings raise ValueError which propagates as 500 Internal Server Error. Wrap in try-except ValueError and raise HTTPException(400, detail={'error': {'code': 'INVALID_DATE', 'message': 'date_from must be ISO 8601 format'}}).
acceptanceCriteria: Invalid date_from or date_to returns 400 with INVALID_DATE error code
Valid ISO dates still work correctly
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Input validation at system boundary — prevents noisy 500 errors in logs.

userStories.title: Fix KanbanBoard default filters={} causing unnecessary re-renders
userStories.description: frontend/src/components/KanbanBoard.jsx line 66: the default parameter `filters = {}` creates a new object reference on every render, defeating React.memo. Move the default to a module-level constant (const DEFAULT_FILTERS = {}) and use that as the default value. This ensures referential equality when no filters are passed.
acceptanceCriteria: Default filters uses a module-level constant, not inline {}
KanbanBoard does not re-render when parent re-renders without filter changes
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: Performance — prevents unnecessary re-renders and refetches on large boards.
